chapters.title: Letter, Dr. Seward to Arthur Holmwood

characters.name: Jonathan Harker
characters.role: Solicitor's clerk sent to Transylvania; the first to enter the castle

characters.name: Mina Harker
characters.role: Schoolmistress, then Jonathan's wife; the memory of the whole enterprise

characters.name: Count Dracula
characters.role: The Count. Travels mostly as cargo, in fifty boxes of Transylvanian earth

characters.name: Lucy Westenra
characters.role: Mina's dearest friend; three proposals in one day, and the first victim

characters.name: Professor Van Helsing
characters.role: Amsterdam physician-philosopher, summoned when medicine runs out

characters.name: The hunting party
characters.role: Arthur Holmwood, Quincey Morris and Dr. Seward, travelling together

locations.name: Munich
locations.quote: Left Munich at 8:35 P. M., on 1st May, arriving at Vienna early next morning

locations.name: Vienna
locations.quote: should have arrived at 6:46, but train was an hour late

locations.name: Budapest
locations.quote: The impression I had was that we were leaving the West and entering the East

locations.name: Cluj-Napoca
locations.quote: Here I stopped for the night at the Hotel Royale

locations.name: Bistrița
locations.quote: Bistritz, which is a very interesting old place

locations.name: Tihuța Pass
locations.quote: the Borgo Pass

locations.name: Castle Dracula
locations.novelName: the Count's castle, above the Borgo Pass
locations.note: Stoker never fixes the castle beyond 'on the very edge of a terrible precipice' east of the Borgo Pass. This point is an editorial best guess in the mountains just beyond the pass.
locations.quote: a vast ruined castle, from whose tall black windows came no ray of light

locations.name: Varna
locations.quote: the Demeter, of Varna

locations.name: Royal Crescent, Whitby
locations.quote: Lucy met me at the station, looking sweeter and lovelier than ever, and we drove up to the house at the Crescent in which they have rooms

locations.name: St Mary's churchyard, Whitby
locations.novelName: the churchyard on the East Cliff
locations.quote: This is to my mind the nicest spot in Whitby

locations.name: Tate Hill Pier, Whitby
locations.quote: an immense dog sprang up on deck from below

locations.name: King's Cross station, London
locations.quote: fifty cases of common earth, to be delivered at Carfax
locations.quoteRef: Ch. 8, letter, Billington & Son to Carter, Paterson & Co.

locations.name: Hospital of St. Joseph and Ste. Mary, Budapest
locations.novelName: the Hospital of St. Joseph and Ste. Mary, Buda-Pesth
locations.note: The hospital is Stoker's invention; this point stands in central Pest.
locations.quote: He has had some fearful shock—so says our doctor

locations.name: Exeter
locations.quote: Mr. Hawkins... has taken Jonathan into partnership

locations.name: Hillingham
locations.note: The Westenra house is fictional. Stoker's geography (Hampstead Heath close by, the 17 miles to Purfleet) points to Hampstead; this point is an editorial best guess there.
locations.quote: Hillingham

locations.name: Carfax, Purfleet
locations.note: Stoker places Carfax at Purfleet, Essex, but the estate itself is invented. This point sits by the old chalk quarries at Purfleet.
locations.quote: At Purfleet, on a by-road, I came across just such a place as seemed to be required

locations.name: Dr. Seward's asylum, Purfleet
locations.note: Fictional, but the novel is explicit that it adjoins Carfax — so it is drawn next door.
locations.quote: a lunatic asylum. It is not, however, visible from the grounds

locations.name: Piccadilly
locations.note: The street is real; No. 347 is Stoker's invention. This point stands mid-Piccadilly, near Green Park.
locations.quote: It is the man himself!

locations.name: Highgate Cemetery
locations.note: 'Kingstead' is generally identified with Highgate Cemetery — close to Hampstead Heath, where the 'bloofer lady' walked.
locations.quote: the tomb of the Westenras

locations.name: Hampstead Heath
locations.quote: The Hampstead Horror

locations.name: Chicksand Street, Spitalfields
locations.novelName: 197, Chicksand Street, Mile End New Town
locations.quote: 197, Chicksand Street, Mile End New Town

locations.name: Jamaica Lane, Bermondsey
locations.note: Stoker's 'Jamaica Lane' is usually read as the Jamaica Road area of Bermondsey.
locations.quote: Jamaica Lane, Bermondsey

locations.name: Doolittle's Wharf
locations.note: The wharf is fictional. The Czarina Catherine sailed from the Thames below the Pool; this point is an editorial best guess there.
locations.quote: the Czarina Catherine

locations.name: Charing Cross station, London
locations.quote: we left Charing Cross

locations.name: Paris
locations.quote: got to Paris the same night, and took the places secured for us in the Orient Express

locations.name: Amsterdam
locations.quote: my old friend and master, Professor Van Helsing, of Amsterdam

locations.name: Galați
locations.quote: Czarina Catherine reported entering Galatz at one o'clock to-day

locations.name: Verești
locations.quote: Veresti

movements.note: The diligence to Bukovina, among crossing-themselves fellow passengers
The Count's calèche, driven by a coachman with very red lips
To Whitby with her mother for the summer
Fifty boxes of earth, by Szgany cart and Slovak river-craft, down to the Black Sea
The voyage of the Demeter: her whole crew lost, her captain lashed dead to the wheel
Sleepwalking, in the dead of night
Running after her, barefoot on the East Cliff steps
The escape down the castle wall — six weeks of brain fever later, he is in Buda-Pesth
Fifty boxes of common earth by Great Northern Railway to King's Cross, thence to Carfax
To nurse Jonathan; they marry at his bedside
Summoned by Seward's wire; boat and boat-train
Back for his books — and his garlic
The night the wolf Bersicker was set at the window
Home, married, to Mr. Hawkins's house
Laid, with her mother, in the family tomb
The funeral
Up for Mr. Hawkins's funeral — and there, outside Giuliano's, the Count, grown young
The 'bloofer lady' of the Westminster Gazette
To read Mina's transcript of Jonathan's journal
Back to town; the churchyard work begins
Van Helsing's proof: the coffin is empty
The whole company gathers under Seward's roof
The first raid: consecrated wafer, and rats in their thousands
As mist, through Renfield's window — the attack on Mina
Sterilising the boxes, one by one
Godalming and Morris, off to destroy the Bermondsey boxes
Back to Piccadilly in time for the Count
Cornered, he breaks through the window and is gone
In a box aboard the Czarina Catherine, cleared for Varna — but she slips past to Galatz
The boat train, Dover to Calais
The Orient Express — to Varna, to wait for the Count's ship
The Czarina Catherine has given them the slip
The first prong: by rail, then carriage, to close the Borgo Pass
Carriage and changes of horses, into the snow
The holy circle in the snow; the three sisters destroyed
With Lord Godalming: by steam launch up the Sereth, then the Bistritza
Morris and Seward, riding the river bank; the cart intercepted at sunset
Up the river with the Szgany, in the last box of earth — overtaken as the sun sets
